messages: Hey, you made a choice to block this for a reason. That reason still matters.
Your future self will thank you for closing this tab right now.
This is your brain saying: let's do something better with our time.
You set this boundary. That takes real strength. Honor it.
Back again? Remember why you started. You're stronger than a habit.
Two visits in a row — that's the urge talking, not you. Let it pass.
The craving lasts about 15 minutes. Go for a walk. Seriously.
You've already proven you can resist once. Do it again.
Okay, visit number {count}. At this point you're basically arm-wrestling yourself. Spoiler: the better you wins.
Your willpower is being tested. Plot twist: it's also getting stronger.
Fun fact: every time you close this tab, a neuron somewhere throws a party.
You keep coming back, and I keep blocking. I have infinite patience. Do you?
Visit #{count}. Let's be real — this pattern isn't making you happy. You know that.
You've tried {count} times today. Imagine putting that energy into literally anything else.
I'm not judging. But your screen time report will. Close the tab.
At this point we're old friends. And as your friend: go drink some water and touch grass.
Attempt #{count}. You blocked this for a reason. That reason hasn't changed, even if your impulse has.
{count} times. You're fighting hard today. That means something — it means you haven't given up.
Still here? Good. That means the block is working. The urge will pass. It always does.
I will never get tired of blocking this. Will you get tired of trying? I hope so.
No scrolling right now. Your real life is more interesting than their highlight reel.
Plot twist: nothing happened on {domain} in the last 5 minutes.
The feed will still be there later. Your focus window won't.
You chose focus over FOMO. That's literally a superpower.
Back for more? {domain} doesn't miss you. Your goals do.
You just checked 2 minutes ago. Nothing changed. Except you lost 2 minutes.
Your fingers autopiloted here. Your brain is smarter than your fingers.
Does {domain} really deserve more of your day?
Visit #{count} to {domain}. At this rate, you should ask for a salary.
Imagine explaining to your future self that you tried to open {domain} {count} times today.
Your thumb has muscle memory. Your brain has better things to do.
{domain} engagement team would be thrilled. Don't give them the satisfaction.
{count} attempts at {domain}. This is no longer a habit — this is a hostage situation. Free yourself.
At this point, {domain} owes you rent for living in your head.
You vs. {domain}: Score is {count}-0 in their favor. Time to flip the scoreboard.
Breaking news from {domain}: still the same memes, still the same drama. You're not missing anything.
Attempt #{count}. Listen, {domain} is algorithmically designed to be addictive. You're not weak — you're fighting a billion-dollar machine. Keep fighting.
{count} times today. That's not failure — that's {count} times you were stopped from wasting your time. The system is working.
Still trying? That's okay. Each block saved you about 20 minutes of mindless scrolling. You're welcome.
The average person spends 2.5 hours daily on social media. Today, you're not average.
No videos right now. That 'one episode' always becomes five.
Your watch history can wait. Your to-do list can't.
The algorithm wants your attention. Don't give it away for free.
Whatever you were about to watch — it'll still exist after your work is done.
Back again? The autoplay rabbit hole is deeper than you think.
You already resisted once. That trailer or video will survive without your view.
Netflix didn't pause for you. But you can pause Netflix for yourself.
One video = 10 minutes. 'One more' = 3 hours. Math checks out.
Visit #{count}. You're not bored — you're avoiding something. Go do that thing instead.
Your brain wants dopamine. Try a 5-minute walk instead of a 5-hour binge.
If productivity had a final boss, it would be {domain}. You're winning this fight.
The content creators will still be there tomorrow. Your deadline won't.
Attempt #{count} at {domain}. That's dedication. Now redirect it toward something useful.
You've tried {count} times. Each time I blocked you, I saved you about 45 minutes. You're welcome.
At this rate, you would have watched an entire season by now. Instead, you're being productive.
The recommended section isn't going to recommend you a better life. You have to build that yourself.
{count} attempts. You're in a tug-of-war with one of the most addictive platforms ever built. And you're still standing.
Still here? Good news: every blocked attempt means the system is doing exactly what you asked it to do.
Attempt #{count}. The craving peaks and fades. Ride the wave. It always passes.
You blocked this for a reason. {count} attempts later, that reason is {count} times more valid.
No swiping right now. The right person won't mind if you check later.
Focus on becoming the person someone would swipe right on.
The matches will still be there. Your self-improvement window is now.
Real connections happen offline too. Go make one.
Back to check for matches? Your inbox is the same as 5 minutes ago.
Nobody fell in love in the time between your last two visits. Promise.
Swipe right on your goals today. They'll love you back.
The butterflies in your stomach are just anxiety about checking. Let them settle.
Visit #{count}. Your love life won't improve by refreshing the app 6 times an hour.
Plot twist: the most attractive thing is someone who doesn't need validation from an app.
You know what's more attractive than a dating profile? Someone who's focused and productive.
The algorithm serves you options. Self-improvement serves you results.
Attempt #{count}. At this point, the app is dating you, not the other way around.
{count} visits to a dating app today. The app is getting more of your attention than any actual person would.
If you spent this energy on a hobby, you'd have way better stories for a first date.
The app wants you addicted. Your blocked list wants you free. Choose wisely.
{count} attempts. The dopamine hit of a new match is real — and temporary. Build something lasting instead.
Still here? Here's a truth: confidence built through real achievements is more attractive than any profile photo.
Attempt #{count}. The most important relationship today is the one with your goals.
{count} tries. Each one blocked = time you invested in yourself instead. That compounds.
No gaming right now. Your real-life XP needs grinding too.
Save your progress in real life first, then you can game later.
The lobby will still be there. Your productive hours won't.
Side quests in real life actually pay off. Just saying.
Back for round 2? Your work has a respawn timer of zero. Get to it.
The rank reset isn't going anywhere. Your deadline is.
You vs. Your Goals: Don't let this be a rage quit.
The game doesn't need you right now. But your responsibilities do.
Visit #{count}. You're speedrunning procrastination at this point.
Achievement unlocked: Tried to game {count} times and got blocked every time.
If focus was a game, you'd be on the hardest difficulty right now. Keep going.
Your K/D ratio in real life: {count} blocked attempts, 0 hours wasted. That's a perfect score.
Attempt #{count}. The final boss of productivity is the urge to game. You're almost through.
{count} tries? That's more attempts than most boss fights. Channel that persistence into your work.
Leaderboard update: You're #{count} on the 'Most Persistent Gamer' list. Impressive. Now go be productive.
The patch notes for your life don't include 'unlimited gaming.' Feature working as intended.
{count} attempts. Your dedication to gaming is honestly impressive. Now imagine that dedication applied to your goals.
GG on attempt #{count}. But the real play is closing this tab and doing what matters.
You've hit the attempt cap for today. There's no microtransaction to unlock more. Go be productive.
Attempt #{count}. Legendary persistence. Wrong target. Redirect that energy and you'll be unstoppable.
The news will still be there later. Your focus is needed now.
If something truly important happens, you'll hear about it. Trust me.
Doomscrolling disguised as 'staying informed' is still doomscrolling.
Breaking: Local person decides to be productive instead of reading news. More at 11.
Back again? The headlines haven't changed in the last 3 minutes.
You already know what's happening in the world. Now make something happen in yours.
The comment section will survive without your contribution today.
Refreshing the news won't change it. Working on yourself will change you.
Visit #{count}. You're not staying informed — you're staying distracted.
If the world ends, someone will literally knock on your door. You don't need {domain} for that.
The outrage cycle wants your attention. Your work deserves it more.
Hot take: the most productive people don't check the news {count} times before lunch.
Attempt #{count}. At this point, the news is reading you more than you're reading it.
{count} visits to {domain}. The algorithm now knows your political views, your anxieties, and your lunch break. Maybe take a break from it.
BREAKING: Person blocked from news site {count} times becomes more productive. Shocking development.
The 24-hour news cycle will keep cycling without you. Your goals need you now.
Attempt #{count}. You're doom-scrolling on hard mode. The block is your friend today.
{count} tries. Most of today's news will be irrelevant by next week. Your work today won't be.
Still checking? Here's your news update: You're being incredibly persistent. Channel that somewhere useful.
The news feeds on your attention. Starve it. Feed your ambitions instead.
Your wallet says thanks. Whatever it is, you don't need it right now.
Sleep on it. If you still want it tomorrow, it'll still be there.
Window shopping online is still shopping. Your bank account knows the difference.
The sale isn't going anywhere. But your money might if you open that tab.
Back again? That 'add to cart' dopamine hit isn't worth it.
You've saved money twice now by not opening this site. Keep the streak alive.
The deal of the day is: not spending money. Take it.
Your cart can wait. Your goals can't.
Visit #{count}. {domain} has very smart people whose entire job is making you buy things. Don't let them win.
Retail therapy is temporary. The credit card statement is forever.
Plot twist: the thing you want to buy won't make you as happy as finishing your work will.
{count} shopping attempts blocked = approximately $200 saved. You're welcome.
Attempt #{count}. If you need it that badly, write it down and buy it this weekend. If you forget, you didn't need it.
{count} visits to {domain}. That's not shopping — that's a compulsion. The block is helping you.
Your cart has been abandoned {count} times today. In marketing terms, you're a lost cause. In life terms, you're winning.
Money saved today: priceless. Focus maintained: also priceless. Times blocked: {count}.
{count} attempts to shop. Imagine if those were {count} minutes of deep work instead. The math works out in your favor.
Still here? The best purchase you can make today is investing time in yourself. That's free.
Attempt #{count}. You are more than your consumption habits. Go create something.
{count} times. The things you own end up owning you. Today, you own your focus.
The house always wins. Today, so do you — by not playing.
You blocked this for a reason. Trust the version of you who made that decision.
The only guaranteed win is not gambling. Take it.
Your money is safer in your pocket than on any bet.
Back again? The odds haven't changed. They're still against you.
Two attempts. The urge is strong, but your decision to block was stronger.
No bet today is going to change your life. But staying focused might.
The 'one more bet' mindset is exactly why you blocked this. Stay strong.
Visit #{count}. The gambling site isn't going to give you what you're really looking for.
You've resisted {count} times today. That's not weakness — that's a winning streak.
The only thing you're losing by staying here is the urge. Let it go.
Every blocked attempt is money you kept. Count that as your win today.
Attempt #{count}. This is a fight worth fighting. The urge passes. It always passes.
{count} tries. If you need support, please reach out to the National Problem Gambling Helpline: 1-800-522-4700.
You blocked this because you care about your future. {count} attempts later, that future still matters.
The thrill of a bet lasts seconds. Financial stability lasts a lifetime. Choose wisely.
Attempt #{count}. You're going through something hard today. That's okay. The block is protecting you.
{count} attempts. Please consider talking to someone you trust. You don't have to fight this alone.
Still here at attempt #{count}. That means the blocker is doing exactly what you asked. Trust past-you.
{count} times. You are more than this urge. It will pass. If you need help: 1-800-522-4700.
This site is blocked. You set this boundary — that takes real discipline.
Not today. You've got better things to do.
Blocked. Your focus mode is active and working.
You decided this site wasn't worth your time. Good call.
Back again? Your autopilot brought you here. Your brain knows better.
Second attempt. The habit loop is trying to run. Don't let it.
You already said no once. Say it again. It gets easier.
Blocked again. The system is working exactly as designed.
Visit #{count}. Persistence is great — just point it at the right target.
You vs. Distraction: {count} rounds in. You're still winning.
If stubbornness was a skill, you'd be maxed out. Now go be stubborn about your goals.
Blocked {count} times. Each one is a small victory for your focus.
Attempt #{count}. The urge is real. So is your decision to block this. Trust the decision.
{count} times today. That's {count} moments where you chose long-term over short-term.
Still here? That's okay. The block is doing its job. Go do yours.
{count} blocked visits. You're building discipline in real-time. Keep going.
Attempt #{count}. You haven't given up on blocking it, and I haven't given up on blocking you. Deal?
{count} attempts. Every single one was caught. The system works. Now go work on something that matters.
We're at #{count} now. At this point, checking this site is the real distraction from the distraction.
Still fighting at #{count}? Good. That means you still care about your goals. Don't stop.
